etymology.description: Phonetic 𡍬 with 貝(貝) (bèi, "cowry", "money") -> 士(士) (shì, "scholar") and 四(四) (sì, "four") -> final form
etymology.interpretation: The character 𧶠 originally had 𡍬 as its phonetic component and 貝(貝) (bèi, "cowry") for its semantic component, reflecting trade or commerce. Over time, 𡍬 was simplified to parts resembling 士(士) (shì, "scholar") and 四(四) (sì, "four"), morphing into its current form.
